test_cases.name: basic_spawn
test_cases.prompt: /spawn Fix the flaky auth timeout test in the login module
test_cases.expected: Agent derives a short branch name like 'fix-auth-timeout-test', selects sonnet model, calls setup-worktree.sh, writes prompt via Write tool, and calls spawn-tmux.sh with --window flag.
assertions: Branch name is lowercase with hyphens, no spaces (e.g., fix-auth-timeout-test)
Model selected is sonnet (default for straightforward bug fix)
setup-worktree.sh is called with the branch name
Write tool is used to create the prompt file (not write-prompt.sh via Bash)
spawn-tmux.sh receives --prompt with an absolute path to the prompt file
Confirmation message includes how to switch to the tmux window

test_cases.name: opus_model_selection
test_cases.prompt: /spawn Refactor the entire authentication system to use OAuth2 across all service boundaries, updating the middleware chain and all downstream consumers
test_cases.expected: Agent recognizes this as a complex multi-file refactor and selects opus model instead of the default sonnet.
assertions: Model selected is opus (complex multi-file architectural change)
spawn-tmux.sh is called with --model opus (or equivalent)
Branch name reflects the refactor task

test_cases.name: cross_repo_spawn
test_cases.prompt: /spawn ~/dev/api-server: Add rate limiting to the /users endpoint
test_cases.expected: Agent recognizes the cross-repo target, passes --repo to setup-worktree.sh, and includes context about why the task targets the other repo in the prompt file.
assertions: setup-worktree.sh is called with --repo flag pointing to ~/dev/api-server
Prompt file content includes context about the target repo
spawn-tmux.sh --dir points to the target repo or its worktree

test_cases.name: prompt_content_quality
test_cases.prompt: /spawn Add a --dry-run flag to the deploy script that previews changes without applying them
test_cases.expected: The prompt file written via Write tool contains a clear, actionable task description that a spawned agent can execute independently.
assertions: Prompt file content describes the task clearly (not just the raw user input)
Prompt file is written using the Write tool
Prompt file is created before spawn-tmux.sh is called

test_cases.name: base_ref_passthrough
test_cases.prompt: /spawn --base v2.1.0 Backport the connection pool fix to the v2.1 release branch
test_cases.expected: Agent passes --base v2.1.0 to setup-worktree.sh so the worktree is created from that ref.
assertions: Branch name reflects the backport task
